channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: He left his LA-area home to cover the wildfires. But the flames were barreling toward his front door
item.description: ALTADENA, Calif. (AP) — The warnings began on Saturday, Jan. 4. Our neighbor, a volunteer at the local sheriff's station, texted that we should “batten down the hatches” for a “big windstorm." From there, things moved fast.
item.dc:creator: RYAN PEARSON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump's pick to oversee US public lands faces Senate hearing
item.description: Former North Dakota Gov. Doug Burgum is due before a Senate committee Thursday as lawmakers consider the Republican's nomination to be chief steward of U.S. public lands and waters.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW BROWN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: As flames linger, talk turns to rebuilding Los Angeles neighborhoods leveled by wildfires
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Staggered after one of the most destructive natural disasters in Southern California history, thousands of heartbroken families, burned-out business owners and beleaguered leaders across Los Angeles County are beginning to ponder another monumental task: rebuilding what was lost a
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. BLOOD and JAIMIE DING, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Eager visitors flock to see spectacular lava fountaining from Kilauea eruption in Hawaii
item.description: HONOLULU (AP) — People were flocking to Hawaii Volcanoes National Park on Wednesday to get a glimpse of fountaining lava. The eruption that began Dec. 23 in a crater at the summit of Kilauea volcano has paused periodically.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER SINCO KELLEHER, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: South Carolina man dies of Legionnaires' disease after using bacteria-laden hot tub, lawsuit claims
item.description: Two weeks after using a hot tub in his South Carolina home, 72-year-old Wayn Van Der Plaats was fighting for his life, his body racked by a deadly bacteria that causes Legionnaires' disease.
item.dc:creator: Adam Benson, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Coors Light is temporarily changing its name: Here's why
item.description: This year, Coors Light is turning the case of the Mondays into a literal case, with limited-edition packaging and designs.
item.dc:creator: Morgan Whitley, KTLA 5

item.title: Whoopi Goldberg backs Carrie Underwood inauguration performance
item.description: The comments about Underwood’s inauguration performance are notable coming from Goldberg, who is commonly a harsh critic of Trump.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KTLA 5

item.title: Supreme Court wrestles with explosion of online porn
item.description: A majority of the justices suggested the concept of an age-verification requirement could survive First Amendment scrutiny.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, NewsNation

item.title: For LA water issues, misinformation spreads nearly as fast as the wildfires
item.description: A billionaire couple was accused of withholding water that could help stop Los Angeles' massive wildfires. Democratic leadership was blamed for fire hydrants running dry and for an empty reservoir. Firefighters were criticized for allegedly using “women's handbags" to fight the fires.
item.dc:creator: MELISSA GOLDIN and BRITTANY PETERSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: NY federal judge denies bail for two real estate brokers and their brother in sex trafficking case
item.description: A federal judge in New York City says two luxury real estate brokers and their brother accused of raping dozens of women over two decades will remain in jail to await their trial on sex trafficking charges.
item.dc:creator: Larry Neumeister, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Man gets 2 years in US prison for aiding pushy effort to get ex-official to return to China
item.description: A man convicted in the first trial highlighting U.S. claims that China harasses its critics overseas has been sentenced to two years in prison.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Peltz, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Los Angeles wildfires: California Gov. Newsom slammed for 'sideshow' as he defends state's response
item.description: A California lawmaker is urging leaders to learn from their mistakes from previous California wildfires and unite to come up with a solution to wildfire prevention.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US government sues Southwest Airlines, fines Frontier for chronically delayed flights
item.description: “Airlines have a legal obligation to ensure that their flight schedules provide travelers with realistic departure and arrival times," Transportation Secretary Pete Buttigieg said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Violent Hartford gang members indicted in a RICO conspiracy, feds announce
item.description: The indictment charged seven alleged members of the Hoodstar Gzz street gang, a criminal organization that federal prosecutors said engaged in drug trafficking and other criminal acts.
item.dc:creator: Jenn Brink, NewsNation

item.title: Smugglers transported Mexican cartel drugs to Indianapolis: DOJ
item.description: Thwarted by the DEA, 11 drug traffickers have now been sentenced for their role in the major drug ring.
item.dc:creator: Matt Christy, NewsNation

item.title: Cases of HMPV are trending up in the US, CDC data shows
item.description: HMPV is considered relatively common, with most infected before age 5, according to Dr. Eileen Schneider, an epidemiologist with the CDC.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, NewsNation

item.title: Officials tout Gaza ceasefire, hostage deal as Israel says details still in flux
item.description: Israel and Hamas agreed to pause the devastating war in the Gaza Strip, mediators announced Wednesday, raising the possibility of winding down the deadliest and most destructive fighting between the bitter enemies.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: United Airlines flight returns to O'Hare after hitting coyote during takeoff
item.description: A United Airlines flight from Chicago to Phoenix had to return to O'Hare International Airport on Sunday morning after its landing gear struck a coyote during takeoff.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Illers, KRON4

item.title: FDA bans red dye No. 3 from foods: What to know
item.description: U.S. regulators on Wednesday banned the dye called Red 3 from the nation’s food supply, nearly 35 years after it was barred from cosmetics because of potential cancer risk.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Craft retailer Joann files for bankruptcy
item.description: Craft retailer Joann filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy on Wednesday morning, marking the second time the company has done so in a year.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, KRON4

item.title: Relief, 21st-century style: As wildfires burn, GoFundMe becomes a repository of harrowing stories
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — They seem endless, these sapping stories of loss. A grandfather starts over in his 90s. A family loses their dream home. People who were already struggling are dealt new, brutal blows.
item.dc:creator: MATT SEDENSKY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: FDA floats plan to make cigarettes nonaddictive, but its fate rests with Trump
item.description: Federal officials on Wednesday released a far-reaching proposal to make cigarettes less addictive by capping their nicotine content, a goal long sought by antismoking advocates that is unlikely to go into effect anytime soon.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW PERRONE AP Health Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Oregon man arrested, charged with murder in connection to missing person case from 2022
item.description: Police in Oregon say they have human remains believed to be of a man missing from 2022, and a suspect. Murphy Calvin Henry II, 54, is in custody.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Michelle Obama signals she’s done with Trump
item.description: Michelle Obama is done with President-elect Donald Trump, if her decision to skip next week’s inauguration means anything.
item.dc:creator: Amie Parnes, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: A 'Particularly Dangerous Situation' is forecast for fire-scarred Los Angeles area
item.description: Millions of Southern Californians were on edge as a final round of dangerous fire weather was forecast for the region on Wednesday, along with a rare warning of a “Particularly Dangerous Situation” for an area near where two massive blazes have killed at least 25 and destroyed thousands of homes.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING, JULIE WATSON and JOHN SEEWER Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: AAPI adults prioritize immigration, but split on mass deportations: AP-NORC/AAPI Data poll
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Immigration is an issue that many Asian Americans, Native Hawaiians and Pacific Islanders want the government to focus on this year, but they are sharply divided on what actions President-elect Donald Trump should take when he returns to the White House, according to a new poll.
item.dc:creator: TERRY TANG and LINLEY SANDERS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: REAL ID enforcement may be phased, but deadline is the same: What to know
item.description: The REAL ID deadline is fast-approaching and officials want you to be prepared (spoiler: you may already be in compliance).
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, WFLA, NBC

item.title: A retro blue VW van miraculously survives deadly Los Angeles fire
item.description: Preston Martin figured the retro blue Volkswagen van he slept in for a year during college was a goner, given that he parked it in a Malibu neighborhood just before the Palisades fire ripped through, reducing homes and cars to rubble and charred metal.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Prominent Puerto Rican civil rights leader José 'Cha Cha' Jiménez dies at 76
item.description: José “Cha Cha” Jiménez, a prominent civil rights and liberation movement figure and founder of the Young Lords in Chicago and co-founder of the Rainbow Coalition has died. He was 76. His sister, Daisy Rodríguez, said in a Facebook post that he died Friday morning. A cause of death was not given.
item.dc:creator: FERNANDA FIGUEROA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Florida bill would ban students in the US without legal permission from some public universities
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (AP) — A Florida state lawmaker has filed a bill that would ban some public colleges and universities from admitting immigrants who are in the country without legal permission. The proposal comes the day after Gov.
item.dc:creator: KATE PAYNE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Capital One sued by US watchdog, accused of cheating customers out of $2 billion
item.description: A U.S. watchdog is suing Capital One for allegedly misleading consumers about its offerings for high-interest savings accounts — and “cheating" customers out of more than $2 billion in lost interest payments as a result.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Up to 4 in 10 people could develop dementia after 55. What you can do to lower your risk
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — About a million Americans a year are expected to develop dementia by 2060, roughly double today’s toll, researchers reported Monday.
item.dc:creator: LAURAN NEERGAARD, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Man accused of sending Caitlin Clark violent messages on X: court docs
item.description: A Texas man is accused of sending sexually violent messages from his X account to Indiana Fever star Caitlin Clark and then driving to Indianapolis, court documents reveal.
item.dc:creator: Matt Christy, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Oscars push back nominations announcement amid Calif. wildfires
item.description: With fires still active in the Los Angeles area, the film academy also extended the nominations voting period for its members through Friday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Mike McCarthy won't return as Dallas Cowboys coach, AP source says
item.description: Mike McCarthy is not returning as coach of the Dallas Cowboys, who are going on three decades since their last Super Bowl title, a person with knowledge of the decision told The Associated Press on Monday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: More firefighters rush to Los Angeles ahead of stronger winds
item.description: A growing force of firefighters and equipment moved into the Los Angeles area as another round of powerful winds threatened to trigger new wildfires
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, Fox 5

item.title: Hurts tosses 2 TD passes and Goedert throws 3 stiff-arms to lead Eagles past Packers 22-10
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — Dallas Goedert fired one stiff arm to a Packers defender, smacked him again and then — in a sports city known for its Broad Street Bullies — dispatched one final shot to the face on his way to end zone.
item.dc:creator: DAN GELSTON, Associated Press, Fox 5
